title: Untrusted Inputs, Integrity, Privacy, Release, Rollback, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Integrity Purpose
summary: A release analyzes arbitrary paths without limits, logs text excerpts, follows swapped symlinks, treats a digest as authenticity, cannot reproduce its wheel, and has no rollback or incident drill. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the integrity purpose increment in production Bookbot release and residual-risk…
objectives: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
Keep reports and logs to authorized metrics and safe identifiers, with explicit excerpt, retention, deletion, and sharing policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-integrity-purpose — Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-security-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: an assistive-technology terminal review.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: replace an ASCII word with a canonically equivalent combining sequence and compare the declared counts.
def worked_bookbot_security_recovery_defense_2(correctness: bool = True, accessibility: bool = True, security: bool = True, performance: bool = True, reproducible_artifact: bool = True, rollback: bool = True, recovery: bool = True, residual_risk_owned: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "corpusworked2",
        "policy2dekrow",
        "revision7417",
        "commandworked27417",
        "report2dekrow7417",
        "test7417worked2",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    gates = (correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducible_artifact, rollback, recovery)
    passed = sum(1 for gate in gates if gate)
    if not correctness or not accessibility:
        return False, passed, "quality-gates"
    if not security or not performance:
        return False, passed, "risk-gates"
    if not reproducible_artifact or not rollback or not recovery:
        return False, passed, "release-gates"
    if not residual_risk_owned:
        return False, passed, "unowned-risk"
    assert passed == len(gates)
    return True, passed, "production-defense"

Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Defend the Integrity Purpose model · Theory Integrity Purpose.
steps.why: Retrieving Integrity Purpose through the concrete production Bookbot release and residual-risk defense case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Bookbot and Python Defend the Integrity Purpose model · Theory Integrity Purpose, keep Integrity Purpose connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Integrity Purpose model · Theory Integrity Purpose, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Integrity Purpose precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Integrity Purpose model · Theory Integrity Purpose, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 7331 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend an immutable Bookbot release across quality, accessibility, security, capacity, rollback, recovery, and residual risk; keep stdout machine-safe, diagnostics on stderr, status explicit, and every signal understandable without color; then split one word and one UTF-8 sequence across stream chunks and compare batch output byte-for-byte. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use a modern digest for content identity while distinguishing accidental-corruption evidence from authenticated provenance.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Integrity Purpose model · Theory Integrity Purpose, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: A matching SHA-256 digest proves who supplied a text.
